# ION-16032 Booking Template Jetty 12 URI Path Separator Fix — Design Document

## Requirements

### Jira Tickets

| Key | Summary | Type | Priority | Status | Assignee |
|-----|---------|------|----------|--------|----------|
| ION-16032 | BK-Unable to Open Template having slash (/) | External Defect | Medium | Open | Ananya Antony |
| PDSUPPORT-203319 | User not able to open the booking template [CSR13152906] | Support Request | Medium | In Progress | Ananya Antony |

### Support Tickets

| Key | Summary | Priority | Status |
|-----|---------|----------|--------|
| PDSUPPORT-203319 | User saijai.yamsri@dsv.com not able to open booking template — 400 Bad Request in network console | Medium | In Progress |

### Summary

After upgrading Jetty from 11.x to 12.x (via Dropwizard 5.0.1, Jetty 12.1.9), users cannot open booking templates whose names contain a forward slash (`/`). Template names like `"Indorama Polyester to MXZLO /HAPAG"` and `"TAURUS MANUFACTURING TO AUMEL_MELBOURNE, AUSTRALIA/ANL"` trigger an HTTP 400 "Ambiguous URI Path Separator" error. This fix configures **both** Jetty compliance layers (connector-level `UriCompliance` AND ee10 Servlet-level `decodeAmbiguousURIs`) to accept encoded forward slashes (`%2F`), and updates the JAX-RS path annotation to route them correctly.

**Status:** ✅ Verified working in QA (2026-06-17) — both Postman and browser (CORS preflight + PUT/GET).

### Technology Stack

| Component | Technology |
|-----------|-----------|
| Framework | Dropwizard 5.0.1 |
| Servlet Container | Jetty 12.1.9 |
| JAX-RS | Jersey (via Dropwizard) |
| Build | Maven |
| Database | DynamoDB (template storage — unchanged) |

---

## Assumptions and Open Issues

| # | Item | Type | Status | Resolution |
|---|------|------|--------|------------|
| 1 | Only the `/` character is affected by the Jetty 12 compliance change; other special characters (spaces, ampersands, parentheses, etc.) continue to work without modification. | Assumption | Resolved | Verified via character impact analysis |
| 2 | `UriCompliance` is a per-connector Jetty setting and cannot be scoped to a single endpoint path. The relaxation is therefore connector-wide. | Assumption | Resolved | Documented; other endpoints remain protected by their default `[^/]+` JAX-RS regex |
| 3 | No existing clients send `%2F` to endpoints other than `/template/{name}`. | Assumption | Resolved | Other endpoints use `[^/]+` regex — encoded slash → 404 from Jersey, same rejection as before |

---

## High Level Design

### Architectural Overview

The booking module's Template functionality uses a JAX-RS resource (`TemplateResource`) to expose CRUD endpoints for user-defined booking templates. Template names are passed as URL path parameters.

Jetty 12 enforces URI compliance in **two independent layers**, both of which must be configured:

```
┌─────────┐      ┌──────────────────┐      ┌──────────────────┐      ┌──────────────────┐      ┌──────────────┐
│  Client  │─────▸│   Jetty 12       │─────▸│  ee10 Servlet    │─────▸│  Jersey / JAX-RS │─────▸│  Template    │
│ (Browser │      │  HTTP Layer      │      │  Layer           │      │  Routing Layer   │      │  Resource    │
│  / API)  │      │  UriCompliance   │      │  decodeAmbiguous │      │  @Path matching  │      │  + Service   │
└─────────┘      └──────────────────┘      └──────────────────┘      └──────────────────┘      └──────────────┘
                    Layer 1: Connector         Layer 2: Servlet          Layer 3: JAX-RS
                    (rejects %2F if not        (rejects ANY ambiguous    (routes path
                     in allowed set)            violation if decode       to method)
                                                not enabled)
```

### Data Flow — Before vs After

**Before (broken):**
```
Client: GET /booking/template/Name%20%2FHAPAG
  → Jetty 12: detects %2F → AMBIGUOUS_PATH_SEPARATOR violation
  → HTTP 400 Bad Request (request never reaches JAX-RS)
```

**After (fixed):**
```
Client: GET /booking/template/Name%20%2FHAPAG
  → Jetty 12 HTTP Layer: %2F allowed (AMBIGUOUS_PATH_SEPARATOR in UriCompliance)
  → Jetty 12 ee10 Servlet: decodeAmbiguousURIs=true → passes through
  → Jersey: @Path("/{name: .+}") matches, captures name = "Name /HAPAG"
  → TemplateResource.getTemplate() → TemplateService → DynamoDB
  → HTTP 200 OK
```

---

## Low Level Design

### Key Components and Changes

| # | Component | Location | Purpose | Key Changes |
|---|-----------|----------|---------|-------------|
| 1 | `TemplateResource` | `booking/src/main/java/.../template/TemplateResource.java` | JAX-RS resource for template CRUD | Changed `@Path("/{name}")` to `@Path("/{name: .+}")` on GET, PUT, DELETE methods |
| 2 | `BookingApplication` | `booking/src/main/java/.../BookingApplication.java` | Application bootstrap and server config | Added `configureUriCompliance()` postSetupHook with two sub-methods: `applyUriCompliance()` (connector layer) and `applyServletAmbiguousUriDecoding()` (ee10 servlet layer) |
| 3 | `BookingApplicationUriComplianceTest` | `booking/src/test/java/.../BookingApplicationUriComplianceTest.java` | Unit tests for UriCompliance config | Verifies exactly `AMBIGUOUS_PATH_SEPARATOR` is relaxed, security protections preserved, and servlet layer is configured |
| 4 | `TemplateResourceTest` | `booking/src/test/java/.../template/TemplateResourceTest.java` | Unit tests for template resource | Added 3 nested test classes: slash names, non-slash regression, JAX-RS routing verification |

### Guice Module Loading Order

N/A — no changes to module loading.

### AWS Services Used

N/A — no changes to AWS service interactions. Template storage (DynamoDB) is unchanged.

### DynamoDB Changes

N/A — no table, annotation, or schema changes.

### Component Interaction Flow

```
┌──────────────┐    ┌──────────────────────┐    ┌──────────────────────┐    ┌──────────────┐
│   Jetty 12   │    │  ee10 Servlet Layer  │    │  Jersey / JAX-RS     │    │  Template    │
│   HTTP Layer │    │  (ServletContext-    │    │  Routing             │    │  Resource    │
│              │    │   Request)            │    │                      │    │              │
├──────────────┤    ├──────────────────────┤    ├──────────────────────┤    ├──────────────┤
│ 1. Parse URI │    │ 3. Check hasViola-   │    │ 5. Match @Path       │    │ 7. Call      │
│              │    │    tions() → YES     │    │    ("/{name: .+}")   │    │    Template  │
│ 2. Check     │    │                      │    │                      │    │    Service   │
│    UriComp-  │    │ 4. isDecodeAmbig-    │    │ 6. Extract name      │    │              │
│    liance    │    │    uousURIs()=true    │    │    (URL-decoded)     │    │ 8. Return   │
│    → ALLOW   │    │    → PASS THROUGH    │    │                      │    │    template  │
│              │    │                      │    │                      │    │              │
└──────┬───────┘    └──────────┬───────────┘    └──────────┬───────────┘    └──────────────┘
       │                       │                           │
       │ Layer 1 fix:          │ Layer 2 fix:              │ Layer 3 fix:
       │ UriCompliance.with(   │ setDecodeAmbiguous-       │ {name: .+}
       │ AMBIGUOUS_PATH_SEP)   │ URIs(true)               │
```

## UI

N/A — no UI changes. The frontend's existing template API calls work as before.

---

## API Architecture

### Endpoints Affected

| Method | Path | Description | Changes |
|--------|------|-------------|---------|
| GET | `/booking/template/{name}` | Get a specific template by name | `{name}` → `{name: .+}` (allows `/` in name) |
| PUT | `/booking/template/{name}` | Save/create a template | `{name}` → `{name: .+}` (allows `/` in name) |
| DELETE | `/booking/template/{name}` | Delete a template | `{name}` → `{name: .+}` (allows `/` in name) |
| GET | `/booking/template` | List all templates | Unchanged |

### Request/Response Changes

No changes to request body, response body, headers, or status codes. The only change is that URL paths containing encoded forward slashes (`%2F`) are now accepted instead of rejected.

### JAX-RS `@Path` Regex Explanation

The `@Path` template syntax is `{parameterName : regex}`:

- **Before**: `@Path("/{name}")` — implicit regex `[^/]+` (any chars except `/`)
- **After**: `@Path("/{name: .+}")` — explicit regex `.+` (any chars including `/`)

The colon `:` is a separator between the parameter name and its matching regex. `@PathParam("name")` binding is unchanged. For names without `/`, both regexes match identically.

---

## Configuration

### Model Level Configuration

N/A — no YAML configuration changes.

### Stack Level Configuration

| Setting | Before | After | Scope |
|---------|--------|-------|-------|
| Jetty `UriCompliance` | `DEFAULT` (rejects `%2F` in paths) | `DEFAULT` + `AMBIGUOUS_PATH_SEPARATOR` (allows `%2F`) | All application and admin HTTP connectors |
| Servlet `decodeAmbiguousURIs` | `false` (rejects any ambiguous URI at Servlet layer) | `true` (decodes and passes through to Jersey) | Application `ServletContextHandler` |

Both are set programmatically via a `postSetupHook` in `BookingApplication.configureUriCompliance()`. No YAML changes needed.

### Professional Services/System Integrator Configuration

N/A — no customer-facing configuration changes.

| `BookingApplication` | INFO | "Configured Jetty ee10 servlet layer to decode ambiguous URIs for template names (ION-16032)" | Startup — servlet layer configured |
| `BookingApplication` | WARN | "Server factory is not DefaultServerFactory; UriCompliance was NOT applied…" | Startup — if non-default server factory (defensive guard) |

### Event Publishing

N/A — no event publishing changes.

## Installer Changes

---

## Impact on Current Application

### Runtime Behavior

- **Template names with `/`**: Now work correctly (previously HTTP 400)
- **Template names without `/`**: No change in behavior
- **Other endpoints**: No functional change. Encoded `%2F` in other endpoint paths now returns 404 from Jersey instead of 400 from Jetty — same rejection, different layer/status code

### Performance

No performance impact. `UriCompliance` checking is a lightweight bitmask operation during URI parsing.

### Deployment

Standard rolling restart. No data migration, no configuration changes, no coordinated deployment needed.

---

## Resiliency

The `configureUriCompliance` hook includes a defensive guard: if the server factory is not `DefaultServerFactory` (e.g., `SimpleServerFactory`), it logs a WARN instead of silently failing. This prevents a confusing regression in alternative deployment configurations.

---

## Temporary object cleanup, temporary files cleanup

N/A

## Impact on Tools

N/A

---

## Impact on Other Components

**Cross-module impact**: None. The `UriCompliance` change is local to the booking module's server connectors. Other modules (network, auth, registration, etc.) have their own server instances and are unaffected.

**Shared library impact**: None. No changes to `mercury-services-commons`.

**Other services**: None. The fix is purely server-side within the booking module.

---

## Backwards Compatible

**Yes — fully backwards compatible.**

- No API contract changes (same endpoints, same path structure)
- No request/response body changes
- Template names without `/` work identically
- No data migration needed
- Can be deployed independently without coordinating with other services
- Clients (frontend, API consumers) require no changes

---

## Unit Test Plan

### New Tests

| # | Test Class | Test Method | Coverage |
|---|-----------|-------------|----------|
| 1 | `BookingApplicationUriComplianceTest` | `bookingUriCompliance_relaxesExactlyAmbiguousPathSeparatorOnTopOfDefault` | Verifies only `AMBIGUOUS_PATH_SEPARATOR` is relaxed on top of Jetty `DEFAULT` |
| 2 | `BookingApplicationUriComplianceTest` | `bookingUriCompliance_keepsPathTraversalAndEncodingProtections` | Asserts `AMBIGUOUS_PATH_SEGMENT`, `AMBIGUOUS_PATH_ENCODING`, `AMBIGUOUS_PATH_PARAMETER`, `AMBIGUOUS_EMPTY_SEGMENT` remain enforced |
| 3 | `BookingApplicationUriComplianceTest` | `jettyDefault_doesNotAllowAmbiguousPathSeparator_confirmingTheChangeIsMeaningful` | Baseline: stock Jetty 12 rejects `%2F` — proves the relaxation is needed |
| 4 | `BookingApplicationUriComplianceTest` | `applyUriCompliance_setsBookingComplianceOnEveryHttpConnector` | Wiring: compliance is set on both app and admin connectors via `DefaultServerFactory` |
| 5 | `BookingTemplateEncodedSlashServletLayerTest` | `encodedSlashTemplateName_reachesServlet_withFullProductionConfig` | **In-container test (Layer 1 + 2):** stands up real Jetty 12 + ee10 servlet, sends `GET /booking/template/a%2Fb` → asserts 200. Fails if either fix layer is reverted. |
| 6 | `BookingTemplateEncodedSlashServletLayerTest` | `encodedSlashTemplateName_rejectedWith400_whenServletLayerNotConfigured` | **Documents the QA defect:** with only connector fix (no `setDecodeAmbiguousURIs`), ee10 servlet layer still rejects `%2F` with 400 — reproduces the exact bug seen in QA. |
| 7 | `BookingTemplateEncodedSlashServletLayerTest` | `ambiguousPathSegment_stillRejected_evenWithServletDecodingEnabled` | **Security guard:** path-traversal (`%2e%2e`) is still rejected with 400 even with servlet decoding enabled — proves `AMBIGUOUS_PATH_SEGMENT` protection is intact. Fails if someone relaxes connector compliance to `LEGACY`. |
| 8 | `TemplateResourceTest` (nested: `WhenTemplateNameContainsSpecialCharacters`) | `getTemplate_pathAnnotation_shouldAllowSlashesInTemplateName` | Reflection: `@Path` regex matches slash-containing names |
| 9 | `TemplateResourceTest` (nested: `WhenTemplateNameContainsSpecialCharacters`) | `saveTemplate_pathAnnotation_shouldAllowSlashesInTemplateName` | Same for PUT |
| 10 | `TemplateResourceTest` (nested: `WhenTemplateNameContainsSpecialCharacters`) | `deleteTemplate_pathAnnotation_shouldAllowSlashesInTemplateName` | Same for DELETE |
| 11 | `TemplateResourceTest` (nested: `WhenTemplateNameContainsSpecialCharacters`) | `getTemplate_shouldHandleSpecialCharactersInName` (parameterized, 9 values) | Resource method with `/`, spaces, `&`, `()`, `,`, `_`, `-` in name |
| 12 | `TemplateResourceTest` (nested: `WhenTemplateNameContainsSpecialCharacters`) | `deleteTemplate_shouldHandleSlashInName` (parameterized, 2 values) | Delete with Jira ticket sample names |
| 13 | `TemplateResourceTest` (nested: `WhenTemplateNameDoesNotContainSlash`) | `pathAnnotation_shouldStillMatchNamesWithoutSlash` | Regression: regex still matches simple names |
| 14 | `TemplateResourceTest` (nested: `WhenTemplateNameDoesNotContainSlash`) | `getTemplate_shouldWorkWithNamesWithoutSlash` (parameterized, 12 values) | Regression: GET with various non-slash names |
| 15 | `TemplateResourceTest` (nested: `WhenTemplateNameDoesNotContainSlash`) | `saveTemplate_shouldWorkWithNamesWithoutSlash` (parameterized, 3 values) | Regression: PUT with non-slash names |
| 16 | `TemplateResourceTest` (nested: `WhenTemplateNameDoesNotContainSlash`) | `deleteTemplate_shouldWorkWithNamesWithoutSlash` (parameterized, 3 values) | Regression: DELETE with non-slash names |
| 17 | `TemplateResourceTest` (nested: `JaxRsRoutingMatchesDecodedSlashNames`) | `productionTemplate_matchesSlashName_andCapturesFullName` | Jersey `UriTemplate` matcher with real `@Path` annotation and decoded `/` |
| 18 | `TemplateResourceTest` (nested: `JaxRsRoutingMatchesDecodedSlashNames`) | `defaultTemplate_failsToMatchSlashName_reproducingTheRoutingBug` | Proves `{name}` (default `[^/]+`) does NOT match slash names — reproduces the bug |
| 19 | `TemplateResourceTest` (nested: `JaxRsRoutingMatchesDecodedSlashNames`) | `nonSlashNames_routeIdenticallyUnderBothTemplates` (parameterized) | Non-slash names route the same under old and new regex |

### Test Layer Coverage

| Layer | What it protects | Test Class | Faithfulness |
|-------|-----------------|------------|-------------|
| 1 — Connector `UriCompliance` | Jetty HTTP parser allows `%2F` | `BookingApplicationUriComplianceTest` | Tests the `UriCompliance` object and factory wiring directly |
| 2 — ee10 Servlet `decodeAmbiguousURIs` | Servlet layer passes `%2F` through | `BookingTemplateEncodedSlashServletLayerTest` | **Real Jetty 12 server** with `LocalConnector` + ee10 `ServletContextHandler` — the exact stack that rejected `%2F` in QA |
| 3 — JAX-RS `{name: .+}` | Jersey routes `/` in path param | `TemplateResourceTest` (JaxRsRouting nested class) | Jersey `UriTemplate` matcher with production `@Path` annotations |
| Security — path traversal guard | `%2e%2e` still blocked | `BookingTemplateEncodedSlashServletLayerTest` | Same real server — confirms connector compliance blocks traversal even with servlet decoding on |

### Existing Test Impact

No existing tests were modified. All booking unit tests pass.

## Pre-Dev Security

### Security Checklist

| # | Question | Yes/No | Comments |
|---|----------|--------|----------|
| 1 | Does the change handle user-supplied input? | Yes | Template names are user-supplied. The `@Path("/{name: .+}")` regex accepts any non-empty string, but template lookup is scoped to the user's company via DynamoDB partition key. |
| 2 | Is input validated/sanitized at public API boundaries? | Yes | `@NotEmpty @NotNull` on `@PathParam("name")`. Template name is used as a DynamoDB sort key — not as a file path or shell command. |
| 3 | Does the change involve authentication or authorization? | No | Auth unchanged — `SecurityContext` still validates user identity. |
| 4 | Does the change handle sensitive data (PII, credentials, tokens)? | No | Template names are not sensitive. |
| 5 | Are credentials hardcoded or stored in source code? | No | N/A |
| 6 | Does the change use cryptographic operations? | No | N/A |
| 7 | Does the change interact with external systems? | No | DynamoDB interactions unchanged. |
| 8 | Does the change introduce new network endpoints? | No | Existing endpoints modified, no new ones. |
| 9 | Does the change modify access control or permissions? | No | Auth unchanged. |
| 10 | Is the change vulnerable to injection attacks? | No | Template name is used as a DynamoDB key, not interpolated into queries or commands. Path traversal protection (`AMBIGUOUS_PATH_SEGMENT`, `AMBIGUOUS_PATH_ENCODING`) remains enforced by Jetty. |
| 11 | Does the change handle file uploads or downloads? | No | N/A |
| 12 | Does the change modify logging? Could it log sensitive data? | Yes | Adds INFO/WARN log at startup. No sensitive data logged. |
| 13 | Does the change introduce any new dependencies with known CVEs? | No | No new dependencies. |
| 14 | Has the OWASP Top 10 been considered? | Yes | A01 (Broken Access Control): N/A — auth unchanged. A03 (Injection): N/A — template name used as DB key, not in queries. A05 (Security Misconfiguration): Only `AMBIGUOUS_PATH_SEPARATOR` relaxed; all other URI protections enforced. |

## Deployment Verification (2026-06-17)

### QA — Verified Working ✅

| Test | Method | URL | Result |
|------|--------|-----|--------|
| CORS preflight (browser) | `OPTIONS` | `/booking/template/Arijit-test-ION-16032%2F001` | **200** ✅ |
| Save template (browser) | `PUT` | `/booking/template/Arijit-test-ION-16032%2F001` | **200** ✅ |
| Get template (Postman) | `GET` | `/booking/template/Arijit-test-001/002` | **200** ✅ |
| Save template (Postman) | `PUT` | `/booking/template/Arijit-test-001/002` | **200** ✅ |

### INT — Application Fix Deployed, Upstream Proxy Blocks `%2F`

The fix is deployed (both startup log messages present), but `api-alpha.inttra.e2open.com` has an upstream reverse proxy that returns **404 for any URL containing `%2F`** before the request reaches the booking service. This is an infrastructure difference, not an application issue.

```
QA (works):   Browser → api-beta.inttra.e2open.com → ALB → Booking ECS (forwards %2F intact)
INT (fails):  Browser → api-alpha.inttra.e2open.com → [PROXY] → 404 (never reaches Booking)
```

**Action:** Infra team to configure INT proxy to allow `%2F` passthrough, matching QA's behavior.
